A 3D printer is still on my "Wish-List", as Santa had a few problems, this Xmas just gone,

Andréa and Critters.     ....XxX.....

author: @dudleysmith5111
text: The NACA airfoil you used does work but may I suggest you use the Bergey airfoil. Its coordinates are available for free from the University of Illinois Champagne/Urbana airfoil catalogue online. It is specifically designed for wind turbines operating at these very low Reynold’s Numbers.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate

replies.author: @jamesmoody562
replies.text: @dudleysmith5111 I could not find this airfoil can you give a more detailed location for the files please. Thanks James

author: @TheZitherish
text: A feather is a component of an aerodynamic structure. In use the air flows along its long axis, not across it.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: well you are sort of right - mid wing that would be true but at the far end of the wing that would be wrong - check out a condor soaring

replies.author: @TheZitherish
replies.text:
Ha, yes.
I have made pinwheels using feathers. They don’t last long out in the elements, wear and tear, rot etc. I give them a coarse pitch so they don’t spin too fast. Also, it’s hard to find identical (ish) blades.

author: @Mike-nk3su
text: where can I find the template for the feathers i would like to scale it up so that each feather is about two feet long and then print out a template on multiple pieces of paper and then cut them out of wood or pvc so i can test it out on some hoverboard motors I've been saving to build a generator?  also if anyone reads this that can help me out with information on how i can re-wire or rewind a  hoverboard  motor to make it either single phase ac or dc voltage without having to use a bridge rectifier i would be highly appreciative for any information?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: You have to draw one mate - I covered how to do that in video 2403 - all motors with rotating magnetic fields are inherently AC to get DC out you must rectify it - you can use an electrical method or a mechanical method - the mechanical method is like a commutator and brush set and is usually integral to the structure of the motor. Without being rude from how you put the question can I strongly advise you to read up on how motors and generators work - I am not sure you have grasped the principles yet

author: @TheMadManPlace
text:
Now I ask myself "Why not double or triple the number of feathers?"
Surely that would create more electrical energy?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: no mate it won't - actually one will do just as well as 3 or 6 or 12 - It has very little to do with the number of blades but rather the swept area - more blades gives it 'more grip' so to speak but there is no increase in power
